reasoning: The FRED statistical tool provides a baseline probability of ~0.36 for an increase by August 10, driven by a slight downward linear trend (predicted 1.375 vs threshold 1.378) and weak seasonal performance from the same period last year. However, geopolitical factors push slightly upward against this baseline: there is intense market pressure on the Canadian dollar stemming from a 35% tariff deadline and recent technical momentum breaking above the 1.375 resistance in late July. Because of these fundamental upside risks for USD/CAD, I adjust the short-term probability slightly higher than the statistical prior, estimating 0.38 for the August 10 horizon. Over longer horizons, the impact of current tariff news and technical patterns washes out. Given that currency pairs roughly approximate a random walk over long periods, the likelihood of an increase converges smoothly toward 50% for the more distant resolution dates in 2025 and early 2026.
